Title: Origin and early evolution of the plant terpene synthase family
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-CPP as a monofunctional class II diterpene cyclase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate to ent-copalyl diphosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; class II diterpene cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcCPS was identified from hornwort TPS-c lineage. 2. Biochemical assays showed monofunctional class II activity producing ent-CPP. 3. This supports repeated subfunctionalization of ancestral bifunctional CPSKS into CPS-only enzymes during land plant evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source species name not provided in main text excerpt; gene included among representative nonseed plant TPSs characterized biochemically.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for ent-kaurene and gibberellin-related phytohormone biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces sandaracopimaradiene as a bifunctional diterpene synthase, indicating neofunctionalization within TPS-c.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctionally cyclizes GGPP through class II and class I reactions to form sandaracopimaradiene.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; bifunctional class I/class II diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PcSS is a hornwort TPS-c enzyme. 2. It is bifunctional and produces sandaracopimaradiene rather than ent-kaurene pathway products. 3. This demonstrates neofunctionalization of TPS-c members into specialized metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents secondary-metabolism diversification from ancestral CPSKS-like enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized terpenoid metabolite; ecological function not directly established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-CPP as a monofunctional class II diterpene cyclase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to ent-copalyl diphosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; class II diterpene cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LcCPS is a liverwort TPS-c enzyme with monofunctional CPS activity. 2. It stereoselectively produces ent-CPP. 3. It supports parallel evolution of CPS-only enzymes from ancestral CPSKS.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Retains conserved catalytic base dyad motifs associated with ancestral CPS activity.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for ent-kaurene and gibberellin-related phytohormone biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces kolavenol as a bifunctional-like/putatively bifunctional TPS-c enzyme related to KPP synthase neofunctionalization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP via a rearranged copalyl intermediate to yield kolavenol; proposed through ent-kolavenyl diphosphate intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; neofunctionalized diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OpKOS from liverwort produces kolavenol. 2. It exemplifies TPS-c neofunctionalization away from gibberellin precursor synthesis. 3. Sequence suggests altered catalytic-base chemistry linked to ent-KPP formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states OpKOS is an example of monofunctional KPP synthase lacking class I Mg2+-binding motifs, while product detection as kolavenol reflects dephosphorylation of the intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized terpenoid metabolite; ecological function not directly established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-kaurene as a bifunctional CPSKS, consistent with ancestral gibberellin-related TPS activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; mutational inactivation of class I DDxxD motif; coupling assays with stereospecific KS; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctionally cyclizes GGPP first to ent-CPP and then to ent-kaurene.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; bifunctional class I/class II diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PlCPSKS is a liverwort bifunctional CPSKS producing ent-kaurene. 2. Its activity was verified through motif mutagenesis and coupling assays. 3. It supports the hypothesis that ancestral plant TPS encoded a bifunctional ent-kaurene-producing CPSKS.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In text also written as PICPSKS due to formatting; lacks the histidine linked to synergistic GGPP/Mg2+ inhibition, similar to some other CPSKSs.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of gibberellins and related phytohormones

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-CPP as a monofunctional class II diterpene cyclase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to ent-copalyl diphosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; class II diterpene cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MpCPS is a Marchantia TPS-c enzyme. 2. It is monofunctional and stereoselectively produces ent-CPP. 3. It exemplifies parallel subfunctionalization of ancestral CPSKS to CPS-only enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Retains the CPS catalytic base dyad motifs and the histidine implicated in GGPP/Mg2+ substrate inhibition.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for ent-kaurene and gibberellin-related phytohormone biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces isoabienol as a bifunctional TPS-c enzyme, indicating neofunctionalization of ancestral CPSKS activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctionally converts GGPP through a hydroxylated CPP intermediate to isoabienol.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; bifunctional class I/class II diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OlIAS from moss retains bifunctionality but produces isoabienol rather than ent-kaurene. 2. Sequence changes in the class II catalytic base dyad likely redirect product outcome. 3. This illustrates TPS-c diversification into specialized metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper suggests serine substitution for the ancestral asparagine may cause production of a hydroxylated CPP intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized terpenoid metabolite; ecological function not directly established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-kaurene as a bifunctional CPSKS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; mutational and product profiling analyses; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctionally converts GGPP to ent-CPP and then to ent-kaurene.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; bifunctional class I/class II diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LjuCPSKS is a moss bifunctional CPSKS that produces ent-kaurene. 2. It retains ancestral CPS and KS motifs. 3. Its activity supports conservation of ancestral phytohormone-related TPS function in nonseed plants.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents retained bifunctionality in moss lineage.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of gibberellins and related phytohormones

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-CPP as a monofunctional CPS, indicating early vascular-plant subfunctionalization of ancestral CPSKS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to ent-copalyl diphosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; class II diterpene cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SwCPS from lycophyte is a monofunctional CPS. 2. It produces ent-CPP and supports early subfunctionalization in vascular plants. 3. It exemplifies repeated independent emergence of CPS-only enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cited in text as evidence that loss of KS activity occurred early in vascular plant evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for ent-kaurene and gibberellin-related phytohormone biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-kaurene as a bifunctional TPS-c enzyme/KS-containing enzyme consistent with phytohormone-related activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GGPP via ent-CPP intermediate to ent-kaurene.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; bifunctional diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AfKS from fern produces ent-kaurene. 2. It groups within TPS-c rather than TPS-e/f, unlike most KSs. 3. Its retention of KS-specific motifs supports phytohormone-related function and independent KS evolution in ferns.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text also discusses Asu_TPS1 as a fern KS in TPS-c; naming in excerpt/table is not fully consistent.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor of gibberellins and related phytohormones

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-CPP as a monofunctional CPS in ferns.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP to ent-copalyl diphosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; class II diterpene cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VaCPS is a fern monofunctional CPS producing ent-CPP. 2. It retains ancestral CPS catalytic motifs. 3. It indicates independent emergence of subfunctionalized CPSs in fern lineage.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as evidence of fern-specific subfunctionalization independent from other plant lineages.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Precursor for ent-kaurene and gibberellin-related phytohormone biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces ent-isopimara-8,15-diene as a bifunctional TPS-c enzyme, showing specialized metabolism diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctionally converts GGPP to ent-isopimara-8,15-diene via cyclized diphosphate intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; bifunctional diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ValPS from fern is bifunctional and produces ent-isopimara-8,15-diene. 2. It is not dedicated to phytohormone biosynthesis. 3. This demonstrates neofunctionalization within TPS-c in ferns.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Product listed in table as ent-isopimara 8,15 diene.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized terpenoid metabolite; ecological function not directly established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces cis-kolavenol as a neofunctionalized TPS-c enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctionally cyclizes GGPP to a rearranged diterpenoid alcohol identified as cis-kolavenol.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; neofunctionalized diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MpDTPS6 produces cis-kolavenol. 2. It exemplifies diversification of liverwort TPS-c enzymes into specialized metabolism. 3. Product detection likely reflects dephosphorylation of a diphosphate intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classed as bi# in the table, meaning dephosphorylation of the phosphorylated intermediate was inferred rather than directly demonstrated.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized terpenoid metabolite; ecological function not directly established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces kolavenol as a neofunctionalized TPS-c enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctionally cyclizes GGPP to a rearranged diterpenoid alcohol identified as kolavenol.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; neofunctionalized diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MpDTPS2 produces kolavenol. 2. It illustrates liverwort TPS-c specialization away from ent-kaurene biosynthesis. 3. Product detection is consistent with formation and dephosphorylation of a kolavenyl diphosphate intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marked bi# in table.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized terpenoid metabolite; ecological function not directly established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces terpentedienol as a neofunctionalized TPS-c enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctionally cyclizes GGPP to terpentedienol via a diterpene diphosphate intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-c subfamily; neofunctionalized diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MpDTPS7 produces terpentedienol. 2. It further demonstrates functional diversification in liverwort TPS-c enzymes. 3. Product detection likely reflects dephosphorylation of a phosphorylated intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Marked bi# in table.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized terpenoid metabolite; ecological function not directly established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a monofunctional class II diterpene cyclase producing terpentedienyl-PP/KPP-like rearranged product, demonstrating class I activity loss outside TPS-c.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP as a monofunctional class II diterpene cyclase to a rearranged diphosphate intermediate assigned as terpentedienyl-PP.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-h subfamily; class II diterpene cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MpDTPS5 belongs to TPS-h rather than TPS-c. 2. It has lost class I activity and functions as a monofunctional class II cyclase. 3. This shows parallel evolutionary loss of class I activity outside TPS-c.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text describes it as producing rearranged KPP; table lists terpentedienyl-PP.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized terpenoid intermediate; ecological function not directly established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces levopimaradiene as a bifunctional TPS-h enzyme dedicated to secondary metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctionally converts GGPP through class II and class I cyclization steps to levopimaradiene.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-h subfamily; bifunctional diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HsLS from lycophyte is a bifunctional levopimaradiene synthase. 2. It shares catalytic motif features with conifer TPS-d3 resin acid synthases. 3. It supports either early evolution or parallel evolution of levopimaradiene synthase activity in vascular plants.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains LYS and PCH motifs associated with altered class II catalytic base chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized terpenoid metabolite; ecological function not directly established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces levopimaradiene as a bifunctional TPS-h enzyme dedicated to secondary metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctionally converts GGPP through class II and class I cyclization steps to levopimaradiene.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-h subfamily; bifunctional diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PdLS from lycophyte is a bifunctional levopimaradiene synthase. 2. It mirrors motif features of conifer resin acid synthases. 3. It supports evolutionary convergence or deep conservation of levopimaradiene synthesis in vascular plants.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains LYS and PCH motifs and conserved VSIAL-related class I features discussed in text.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized terpenoid metabolite; ecological function not directly established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a monofunctional class II diterpene cyclase producing 8-endo-CPP in TPS-h, showing loss of class I activity outside TPS-c.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGPP as a monofunctional class II diterpene cyclase to 8-endo-copalyl diphosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-h subfamily; class II diterpene cyclase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCPS from fern produces 8-endo-CPP as a monofunctional class II enzyme. 2. It has lost class I activity along with Mg2+-binding motifs. 3. It demonstrates parallel loss of class I activity in TPS-h lineage.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents monofunctional class II cyclase outside TPS-c.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized diterpenoid intermediate; ecological function not directly established in this paper

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a monofunctional class I TPS-h enzyme producing syn-manool, demonstrating loss of class II activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli modular metabolic engineering system supplying syn-CPP; GC-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts syn-CPP to syn-manool as a monofunctional class I diterpene synthase.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-h subfamily; class I diterpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OjMS is a fern TPS-h enzyme that produces syn-manool. 2. It has lost class II activity and functions only as a class I synthase. 3. This indicates independent loss of class II activity in TPS-h evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text compares it to a previously described lycophyte 16α-hydroxykaurene synthase, indicating likely independent origin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized terpenoid metabolite; ecological function not directly established in this paper
